test_cases.title: Valid login shows the inventory
test_cases.description: A user with valid credentials lands on the inventory page and sees the product list.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: Funnel-entry user journey across form, navigation and rendered inventory — the cross-page behavior only an E2E exercise verifies; this is the canonical smoke path.
preconditions: Demo app is being served locally (the demo driver starts it).
expected_results: The inventory section is shown with the heading "Products".
At least one product is listed.

test_cases.title: Invalid password shows the exact error copy and no session
test_cases.description: An invalid password keeps the user on the login form with the agreed error message "Invalid credentials".
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: The error feedback is user-visible UI behavior tied to a high-severity risk; the exact copy is a business contract with support, so it must be asserted in the rendered page, not at a lower seam.
preconditions: Demo app is being served locally.

steps.action: Fill username and a wrong password.

expected_results: The user remains on the login form.
The error message reads exactly "Invalid credentials".

test_cases.title: Error presentation is readable and appropriately styled
test_cases.description: Subjective visual review of the error state (contrast, placement, tone of the copy).
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: Visual judgment (readability, contrast, tone) is subjective and cheap to eyeball; automating a pixel/style assertion here would be brittle and add no risk coverage beyond TC-002.
preconditions: Demo app is being served locally.

steps.action: Review the error visually on a normal viewport.

expected_results: The error is immediately noticeable, readable, and clearly associated with the form.
